author: world today news
title: Habeck defends legal action against haters on the Internet
text:
November 23, 2024, 12:04 a.m
Berlin. Federal Economics Minister and Green Party candidate for chancellor Robert Habeck has defended his legal action against insults on the Internet. ‚ÄúOf course, as a politician, I have to endure criticism and anger. But I don‚Äôt want to accept the ever-increasing blurring of boundaries,‚Äù Habeck told the ‚ÄúEditorial Network Germany‚Äù (Saturday editions).
The Green politician complained that he was confronted online with terms such as ‚Äúscumbag‚Äù, ‚Äúmiscarriage‚Äù, ‚Äúput against the wall‚Äù, Adolf Hitler comparisons and threats to his family.
Habeck, who had filed a criminal complaint against a man from Bavaria who had called him an ‚Äúimbecile,‚Äù admitted that there were words that could be argued about. ‚ÄúBut we mustn‚Äôt turn the debate around either. Insults and threats are not normal, and I don‚Äôt want to accept them as normal either,‚Äù he said.
The Vice Chancellor pointed out that not only politicians but also volunteers are exposed to insults, threats and hatred online. ‚ÄúThis is bad for our democracy,‚Äù said the Green politician.
Regarding the actions of the Augsburg public prosecutor‚Äôs office, which conducted a house search of the hater because of the ‚Äúimbecile‚Äù statement, Habeck said: ‚ÄúWe live in a constitutional state in which the police, public prosecutors and courts decide on their own responsibility. They decide whether they want to‚Ä¶ For example, whether a house search is proportionate or not.‚Äù
